name: Euclidean Algorithm
statement: The gcd of two integers can be computed by repeated division with remainder.
hint: Use gcd(a,b)=gcd(b,r) where a=bq+r.

name: Binomial Theorem
statement: (x+y)^n = sum_{k=0}^n binom(n,k)x^{n-k}y^k.
hint: Count choices of selecting k y's from n factors.

name: Triangle Inequality
hint: Use geometry or square both sides.

name: Infinitude of Primes
statement: There are infinitely many prime numbers.
hint: Assume finite list and construct product + 1.

name: Bézout Identity
statement: There exist integers x,y such that ax+by=gcd(a,b).
hint: Back-substitute Euclidean algorithm steps.

name: Cauchy–Schwarz Inequality
hint: Consider ||x-ty||^2 ≥ 0.

name: AM-GM Inequality
statement: Arithmetic mean ≥ geometric mean for nonnegative numbers.
hint: Use induction or symmetry and squares.

name: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic
statement: Every integer >1 has a unique prime factorization.
hint: Use Euclid's lemma for uniqueness.

name: Chinese Remainder Theorem
statement: System of congruences mod coprime integers has unique solution mod product.
hint: Construct idempotents via Bézout identity.

name: Fermat’s Little Theorem
statement: If p is prime and p∤a then a^(p−1)≡1 mod p.
hint: Permutation of nonzero residues.

name: Euler’s Theorem
statement: If gcd(a,n)=1, then a^{φ(n)} ≡ 1 (mod n).
hint: Generalize FLT using reduced residue system modulo n.

name: Lagrange’s Theorem (Group Theory)
statement: For finite group G and subgroup H, |H| divides |G| and |G| = [G:H]|H|.
hint: Partition G into disjoint cosets of H.

name: Intermediate Value Theorem
statement: A continuous function on [a,b] takes every value between f(a) and f(b).
hint: Use completeness of real numbers and supremum argument.

name: Mean Value Theorem
statement: There exists c in (a,b) such that f'(c) = (f(b)-f(a))/(b-a).
hint: Apply Rolle’s theorem to f minus secant line.

name: Rank–Nullity Theorem
statement: dim(ker T) + dim(im T) = dim V for linear map T:V→W.
hint: Extend basis of kernel to full basis of V.

name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
statement: Derivative of integral is function; integral of derivative gives net change.
hint: Use limit of difference quotient and MVT.

name: Bolzano–Weierstrass Theorem
statement: Every bounded sequence in R^n has a convergent subsequence.
hint: Use nested intervals or compactness argument.

name: Cauchy Integral Theorem
statement: Integral of holomorphic function over closed curve is zero.
hint: Use triangulation or Goursat’s argument.

name: Cauchy Integral Formula
hint: Subtract removable singularity and apply Cauchy theorem.

name: Maximum Modulus Principle
statement: A nonconstant holomorphic function has no interior maximum of modulus.
hint: Use mean value property or Cauchy formula.

name: Residue Theorem
statement: Contour integrals of meromorphic functions equal 2πi times sum of residues inside the contour.
hint: Remove small disks around poles and apply Cauchy’s theorem.

name: Heine–Borel Theorem
statement: A subset of R^n is compact iff it is closed and bounded.
hint: Use Bolzano–Weierstrass for sequential compactness.

name: Banach Fixed Point Theorem
statement: A contraction on a complete metric space has a unique fixed point.
hint: Iterate and show geometric convergence of distances.

name: Cayley–Hamilton Theorem
statement: Every square matrix satisfies its characteristic polynomial.
hint: Use adjugate identity (λI−A)adj(λI−A)=p(λ)I.

name: Spectral Theorem (Finite-Dimensional)
statement: Normal matrices are unitarily diagonalizable.
hint: Use Schur decomposition and normality condition.

name: Jordan Canonical Form
statement: Every matrix is similar to a Jordan block decomposition.
hint: Use generalized eigenspaces and nilpotent structure.

name: Hahn–Banach Theorem
statement: A bounded linear functional can be extended without increasing norm.
hint: Extend step-by-step or use Zorn’s lemma.

name: Radon–Nikodym Theorem
statement: Absolutely continuous measures admit a density function.
hint: Construct derivative in L1 using approximation.

name: Dominated Convergence Theorem
statement: Pointwise convergence under domination allows exchange of limit and integral.
hint: Apply Fatou’s lemma to g ± f_n.

name: Central Limit Theorem
statement: Normalized sums of i.i.d. variables converge to Gaussian distribution.
hint: Use characteristic functions and Taylor expansion near 0.

name: Gershgorin Circle Theorem
statement: Every eigenvalue of a complex matrix lies in at least one Gershgorin disk.
hint: Look at eigenvector component of maximal magnitude.

name: Riemann Mapping Theorem
statement: Every simply connected proper open subset of C is conformally equivalent to the unit disk.
hint: Use normal families and extremal holomorphic maps.

name: Stokes’ Theorem
statement: Integral of exterior derivative over manifold equals integral over boundary.
hint: Reduce locally to FTC via coordinate charts.

name: Fubini’s Theorem
statement: Double integrals can be computed as iterated integrals under integrability assumptions.
hint: Start with simple functions and extend by approximation.

name: Monotone Convergence Theorem
statement: Increasing sequence of nonnegative measurable functions converges under integral.
hint: Approximate limit from below by simple functions.

name: Fatou’s Lemma
statement: Integral of liminf is ≤ liminf of integrals for nonnegative functions.
hint: Define inf-tail sequence and use monotone convergence.

name: Riesz Representation Theorem (Hilbert Space)
statement: Every bounded linear functional is an inner product with a unique vector.
hint: Use orthogonal decomposition into kernel and complement.

name: Banach–Alaoglu Theorem
statement: The unit ball in the dual of a normed space is weak-* compact.
hint: Embed into product of compact disks and use Tychonoff.

name: Uniform Boundedness Principle
statement: Pointwise bounded family of bounded operators is uniformly bounded in norm.
hint: Use Baire category theorem.

name: Open Mapping Theorem
statement: Surjective bounded linear operators between Banach spaces map open sets to open sets.
hint: Show image of unit ball contains a neighborhood of 0.
